questionHtml:
A molecule of citric acid, C6H8O7, is shown.

The equation for the first dissociation of citric acid in water is

C6H8O7 (aq) + H2O (l) ⇌ C6H7O7− (aq) + H3O+ (aq)

Suggest why the resistance of metals increases with temperature.
markschemeHtml:
increased vibrations of «lattice» ions ✔
increased «probability of» collisions «between electrons and cations» ✔

NOTE: Accept “increases lattice vibrations” for M1.

meta.title: Suggest why the resistance of metals increases with temperature.
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.3.1—A metallic bond is the electrostatic attraction between a lattice of cations and delocalized electrons. Explain the electrical conductivity, thermal conductivity and malleability of metals.
